title: Lap-group LambdaMART ranking head
summary: Pit decisions are partly relative within a race-lap: which cars in the current field are most likely to stop next.
rationale: Pit decisions are partly relative within a race-lap: which cars in the current field are most likely to stop next. Learning-to-rank methods explicitly compare items within a query group, which maps naturally to `qid=(Year,Race,LapNumber)` with binary labels for next-lap pitters.
implementation_hint: Train an `XGBRanker` or LightGBM ranker with groups sorted by `Year,Race,LapNumber`; optionally drop groups with no positives or downweight them. Use the same engineered features, output a rank score per row, and blend that score with the binary classifier probability. Validate only with race-blocked folds.
expected_effect: Adds a different inductive bias that can help on crowded pit windows where absolute thresholds are weak but relative ordering among drivers is strong.
risk: Many lap-groups may contain zero positives, so the ranking signal can be sparse. If qids cross races or include leakage from same-race held-out groups, the gain will be fake.
